pieces.title: CFC Event Manager
pieces.background: Continental Finance needed an internal dashboard to monitor and manage high-volume business event workflows across operational teams.
pieces.process: Developed a fullstack internal dashboard using React, Node.js, TailwindCSS, and C#/.NET, seamlessly integrating frontend and backend systems. Led the design of high-fidelity Figma mockups for multiple end-to-end workflows, shaping the frontend using an iterative methodology to ensure user-centric, intuitive experiences and streamline engineering handoff.
pieces.result: Delivered a near real-time monitoring and management tool for 10K+ daily business events across 100+ operational processes, significantly reducing manual intervention and reducing frontend implementation time by 15%.

pieces.title: Samoth
pieces.background: Samoth needed a portfolio platform to present visual project deliverables in an interactive, gallery-driven experience.
pieces.process: Designed and developed a portfolio site with an interactive gallery structure, building the experience to showcase Samoth's visual work with clarity and navigability.
pieces.result: Launched a live portfolio that provides an interactive gallery for Samoth's visual project deliverables, garnering roughly 500 active users in one year.

pieces.title: Finder
pieces.background: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute needed a centralized lost-and-found platform accessible to students, faculty, and staff across campus.
pieces.process: Engineered Finder in collaboration with four peers, implementing secure user authentication, automated item-matching algorithms, and a responsive UI designed for broad campus adoption.
pieces.result: Shipped a lost-and-found web application deployed for use by 9,000+ students, faculty, and staff.

pieces.title: Berle Farm
pieces.background: Berle Farm's e-commerce site presented opportunities to improve usability, visual clarity, and the overall online shopping experience for customers.
pieces.process: Conducted an end-to-end UI/UX study of Berle Farm's e-commerce site, including heuristic evaluation, user flow analysis, and iterative Figma mockups developed across key shopping workflows.
pieces.result: Delivered a 16-page analysis, dynamic Figma mockups, and a 20-minute mock presentation to stakeholders.

pieces.title: HACKRPI X
pieces.background: HACKRPI X required a refreshed web presence to support event promotion, registration flows, and participant engagement for the 2023 hackathon.
pieces.process: Designed and developed components of the HACKRPI 2023 web application as Team Lead of the Rensselaer Center for Open Source HACKRPI Website Development Team, coordinating implementation across the site.
pieces.result: Contributed core site components to the HACKRPI X web application as team lead of the website development team.
